description: Following revelations of serial infidelity in late 2009, Accenture was the first major corporate partner to drop Tiger Woods from its marketing campaigns. This event forced a public debate on whether corporate sponsors should maintain associations with celebrity brand ambassadors when their personal conduct conflicts with corporate values.

key_events.description: National Enquirer breaks the story on Tiger Woods' affair.
Accenture officially terminates its sponsorship contract, citing brand alignment issues.
Woods announces an indefinite leave of absence from professional golf.
General public sentiment stabilizes as Woods remains out of the public eye for three months.

calibration_notes: This scenario models a rapid decay in brand sentiment following a moral crisis. It is useful for calibrating how quickly corporate entities react to public pressure and how 'pro' sentiment shifts from endorsing a brand association to supporting the brand's 'moral' decision to divest.
